item.title: I've spent 30 years playing and 25 years DMing D&D. Roleplaying is hard... So over time I made this. Questions to get to know the new you better.
item.description:
I based the basic structure off of the Gotham and Proust Questionnaires, along with more D&D/RPG specific questions in between.
In short? It's 72 questions that by the time you're done answering them? You know your character.
I've broken them up into general categories and added/subtracted over the years, but these are what I feel (at least today) will get the best bang for your buck.
I love a good resource share so I'd figured I'd share it with the nerds I know (just remember me fondly to your friends and fellow players)
Does it seem like a lot? Sure. But it beats 17 pages of backstory for a 1st level fighter about how awesome they probably were sometime ago.
If you enjoy this? I'm glad I could help. Questions? Feel free to message. Other than that.... Good luck, and Dovie'andi se tovya sagain, — Midnight.
item.dc:creator: MidnightLibrarian

item.title: A Birthmas Post
item.description: Today is my cakeday ? But I want to take this chance to give a shout out to all my fellow December babies who have their day overshadowed by the spectre of Christmas. I see you and share that pain. Happy Birthday to all that it applies to this month! ??

item.title: Christmas Is Coming Soon

item.dc:creator: Warandpeasofficial

item.title: A personal hero
item.description: Dad hated Carl Sagan. I didn't know who he was. Over time I learned to take serious note of the people that my dad hated, and to seek out their wisdom. What's become clear is there are very smart and capable leaders who time and again have been blocked from leadership roles and have been able to do fuck all about it. Then again, at one time, no one in Europe could imagine a world without kings. Front page edit: Another hero I found the same way was Jonathan Kozol. No one seems to have just "heard of him". People I mention him to either have no idea who he is, or are avid seekers of his insight and recommendations. If you have not heard of him I recommend his books on the manufactured decline of the American education system. It predicts the situation we are in now as a goal that was set 50+ years ago (after quality education resulted in too much freedom for the middle and lower classes From @ItsaBeemer The original interview. : https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=acBRahW5c-A
item.dc:creator: Chewi42

item.title: Keep it to yourself. Hide everything. Good lessons to teach a child, right?
item.dc:creator: hazelismycatdog

item.title: Any system that allows this to happen is broken beyond repair. It needs to be rebuilt from the ground up.
item.description: On the other side of the coin, my home state was successfully sued a number of years back because Child Protective Services REFUSES to do anything until after the kid is dead. In the case one guy who survived foster care in my state (one of his siblings did not survive) was put in a foster home where all the kids were literally kept chained to walls and chemical toilets in a locked, barren room. They were only occasionally allowed to eat, but even then only what they could grab and fit in their mouths in 10-15 minutes, then it was back into the room. This was repeatedly done right in front of CPS workers doing routine checkups on them. At the trial the CPS worker's defense was that they "don't have time to report it to police or do ANYTHING about it."
item.dc:creator: Othagovna

item.description: For your information this is buck angel, a trans man and leyna bloom, a trans woman. I thought it was time to address the fact that conservatives are doing the exact opposite of what they say. They want men in women's bathrooms and women in men's. Trans men are men, trans women are women and the only good Nazi is a dead one.
